escalations.reason: Review found 4 blocking contract-violations clustering on one root cause: the frozen spec (REQ-105/REQ-108) pins fixture provenance to plugin 0.30.0, but the actually-installed plugin at regeneration time was 0.32.0 (gatekeeper dir verified byte-identical between the two; fixtures verified byte-equal to live output — no data drift, purely a provenance-pin defect). Frozen TEST-719 pins the wrong version string; CLAUDE.md:179 also retains a stale pre-v2 PHASE_ORDER phrasing (FINDING-005/012). Any spec amendment requires a loopback to spec per the freeze rule. Decision needed: (A) loop back to spec and re-propagate (amend provenance wording to pin by observable contract, fix TEST-719 in tests, fix CLAUDE.md/CHANGELOG in implement), or (B) human-authorized closure of the version-pin findings as accepted-with-disclosure plus a narrower implement/tests descent for the CLAUDE.md text and TEST-719.
escalations.decision: Option A (Kevin, 2026-07-03): loop back to spec. Amend REQ-105/108 to pin fixture provenance by observable contract (contract_version 2 + byte-equality vs live producer output) recording the producer version READ AT GENERATION TIME, not a spec-time literal; tests phase amends TEST-719 accordingly; implement fixes CLAUDE.md:179 and CHANGELOG wording. Fold the MEDIUM cluster into the same descent: resolution-affix full-text reachability (FINDING-001/011), non-empty resolution guard (FINDING-003 root), orky-pane.md documentation (FINDING-007).

loopbacks.reason: TASK-103 assigns golden-fixture regeneration to the implement phase, but tests/fixtures/orky-contract/* are inside the test-freeze snapshot (testRoots=[tests]) — an implementer regenerating them would trip the freeze guard as a CRITICAL integrity finding. Fixtures are test assets: reassign regeneration to the tests phase (test-designer actor) so the re-freeze captures the v2 fixtures and the implementer never touches tests/.

Frozen-tree mechanical failure surfaced by the A/B implement self-verify: TEST-645 (tests/docs-feature-0010-loopback.test.ts, feature 0010's frozen cross-feature inventory sweep, pattern /...|F10/i) fails because the 0015 test file tests/renderer/orky-pane-resolution-structure.test.ts line 15 comment says 'the shipped F9/F10 OrkyPane' — an undispositioned pattern hit baked into the tests-phase checkpoint. Phase of origin: tests. Fix: reword the comment to '0009/0010' (no pattern token); no assertion changes.
ESC-001 resolved: human chose spec loopback to fix the provenance-pin contract violations (FINDING-005/008/012/013) and fold in the MEDIUM cluster (FINDING-001/011/003/007).

implementer.reason: ESC-001 descent delta (TASK-118..122). Router requested ab (determinism concern) but the Codex runtime was unavailable — 2/2 background Codex tasks earlier today wedged in queue and vanished (implementer arm + cross-check, both recorded); dispatched claude solo rather than stage a third wedge. Gate remains the arbiter.
